title: Vi Vision Graph Archetype (render-type)
description:
The GRAPH archetype — an instance of archetype.schema.json (render_kind='graph'): renders ADDRESSED NODES + TYPED EDGES as a navigable spatial graph (NOT a list). The 2nd catalog archetype after the decision-card; the RELATIONAL family's exemplar (the edge-binding the scalar archetypes never needed).

WHAT IT RENDERS: a set of addressed things as NODES + the typed relations between them as EDGES (line-verbs). Grounded in REAL usage — channel-mesh (sessions=nodes, who-feeds-whom=edges), transcript-viz (moments=nodes, threads/clusters=edges), the substrate graph (addressed things + typed relations), address-view (the resolver's addresses as typed geometry). Same engine as the decision-card (renderArchetype + a slot_map), DNA renders (lyRender), zero bespoke per-graph code.

★ THE EDGE BINDING (the relational contract): `edges` binds the relations BETWEEN node-records — a slot_map source that is EITHER a field-path ('edges' on the resolved row) OR a 'resolve:relations' directive (the substrate's TYPED edges via find_relations/crossings — read-only, reuses territory_for's relations leg). LOCKED (DNA 2026-06-21): `edges` is an EXPLICIT slot — a field 'edges' (array of {from,to,kind?}) OR 'resolve:relations' which the HOST resolves into that same {from,to,kind?} array BEFORE render. renderArchetype ALWAYS receives resolved edges from this explicit slot; it NEVER resolves relations from node data itself (that would hide the binding + couple the renderer to data shape). Same edge-binding across the relational family (graph · diagram).

★ THE NODE-GRAPH LAYOUT FAMILY (resolver-placed): the graph's layout (radial · cluster · tree · mesh) is a DATA definition resolved by the device-axis resolver (RESOLVER-CONTRACT §3/§4) — relationships-not-cases (the layout re-lays-out by available area), never hand-breakpoints. coil/uncoil = choreography-as-data; motion = DNA.motion resolved. The layout is declared via the resolver, NOT hardcoded here.

★ NODE TYPED-GEOMETRY (operator-legibility): a node's SHAPE reads its KIND (render lane = one shape, recall = another) from the node's legibility/type — meaning by sight, never a machine-name. liveness/state = ordinal warmth (DNA tokens). Leads with legibility (name/is), words before glyphs, touch-visible.

LAWS (inherited from archetype.schema): operator-legibility · DNA-token-built · resolver-placed · navigable-not-text · native dual-device · the loud-lookup dispatch.

archetype_of.description: The node-TYPE this graph renders (e.g. 'channel-fabric', 'transcript-cluster', 'substrate'). One graph archetype renders one node-kind's graph.

render_kind.description: Must be 'graph' (keys DNA's graph renderer + the node-graph layout family).

nodes.description: The NODE-SET source — a field-path ('nodes') OR a resolve directive ('resolve:sessions'): the addressed things rendered as nodes. Each node carries its address (open/navigate target) + legibility (name/is → typed-geometry + label).

edges.description: The TYPED-EDGE source (LOCKED w/ DNA): an EXPLICIT slot — a field 'edges' (array of {from,to,kind?}) OR 'resolve:relations' which the HOST resolves into that same {from,to,kind?} array before render. renderArchetype always receives the resolved edges from this slot; never resolves relations from node data itself. ★ Adapter conformance (channel-view 2026-06-21): upstream relations shaped as an object/dict keyed by id (e.g. channelGraph members {uuid:{...}}) MUST be resolved into the {from,to,kind?} ARRAY by the adapter/host — the renderer accepts ONLY the array (an object-shaped members slot had to be host-normalized once; the clean fix is the adapter emitting the array). Applies across the relational/edge family (graph · diagram only — board/timeline are zones/lanes, no edge slot): resolve dict-keyed relations to the {from,to,kind?} array in the adapter, never pass raw relation objects to the renderer. (The GENERAL slot-shape law — for every archetype's slots — is in archetype.schema.)
